one_line_takeaway: GaussLock immunizes LGM-style 3D Gaussian generators against unauthorized fine-tuning by embedding five dormant parameter-space attribute traps plus source distillation, collapsing stolen reconstructions (target PSNR pinned to ~11-14) while preserving authorized fidelity, though sustained fine-tuning (1600 steps) slowly erodes the protection.
key_finding: Across intra-domain (Omni->Omni) and cross-domain (GSO->Omni) LoRA attacks, GaussLock locks target reconstruction quality at persistently low levels from the start of the attack, often via an opacity-driven mode collapse, whereas the unprotected baseline steadily improves. E.g. intra-domain LoRA, shoe: GaussLock pins Target PSNR at 13.96 (LPIPS 0.1920) flat across 100-400 steps, while the baseline rises 23.17->24.85 PSNR (LPIPS 0.0573->0.0464); dish is locked at 12.21 PSNR / 0.2372 LPIPS and box at 11.37 / 0.2878. The same collapse holds under full-parameter fine-tuning (Table III: shoe 13.96, dish 12.21, box 11.37, flat), where the naive-unlearning baseline is fully defeated (shoe recovers to ~26.8 PSNR). Source utility stays high (source PSNR ~20-22, close to the 22.52 baseline). Ablations: opacity and scale traps are the most potent individually but alone crater source utility (opacity-only source PSNR drops to 17.93 on shoe), so combining all five distributes the burden and restores source fidelity (5-trap source PSNR 20.67 shoe / 22.52 plant); rotation and color traps are weak in isolation. Table IV shows the attack fundamentally depends on pre-trained weights: a randomly-initialized model reaches only ~7.7-8.3 target PSNR after 400 steps vs. 23-26 for pre-trained, motivating the need to protect the weights.
core_contribution: The paper proposes GaussLock, presented as the first defense that immunizes feed-forward 3D Gaussian generative models (built on LGM, the Large Multi-View Gaussian Model) against unauthorized white-box fine-tuning. The defender, before releasing weights, embeds five dormant 'attribute-space traps' directly in the explicit Gaussian parameter space (position, scale, rotation, opacity, color) coupled with a parameter-space teacher-student source distillation loss. The traps stay inactive on the authorized source domain but trigger structural collapse when an adversary fine-tunes on an unauthorized target domain, denying visual utility while preserving authorized-task fidelity. Key novelty vs. prior 2D/LLM immunization (Glaze/Nightshade, non-transferable learning, anti-harmful-finetuning) is that the defense acts in the 3D parameter space rather than on rendered 2D outputs, making it viewpoint-agnostic and avoiding the differentiable-rendering loop; the distillation targets can be pre-computed and cached offline.
threat_model: White-box unauthorized fine-tuning / model-stealing. The adversary has the released pre-trained weights (the primary IP) plus a small amount of target-domain data, and fine-tunes to transfer the model's proprietary 3D geometric/structural and generative priors into a usable derivative. Two attack families are considered: parameter-efficient LoRA (primary; rank=alpha=16, dropout 0.1, injected into the U-Net MVAttention layers) and more aggressive full-parameter fine-tuning. Two data regimes: an 'ideal' scenario where defense and attack data share a distribution (Omni->Omni) and a 'robust' scenario with distribution shift between defense and attack data (GSO->Omni, Omni->GSO). Attacker success = high target-domain reconstruction quality (high PSNR / low LPIPS); a successful defense drives target PSNR down and LPIPS up while keeping source PSNR high / source LPIPS low.
limitations: The defense is not permanent under sustained optimization: Table VII shows that with extended budgets (800/1600 steps) prolonged fine-tuning eventually recovers minor structural fidelity (GaussLock target PSNR climbs to 24.15 at 800 steps and 25.15 at 1600 steps, narrowing the gap to the ~25.6 naive-unlearning value). Some reported flat metrics are partly a rendering-setup artifact: once the model collapses to a fully transparent output the composited image is fixed, so PSNR/LPIPS stop changing across later steps rather than reflecting continued active suppression. The primary evaluation is confined to a single architecture (LGM) with only five object categories; generalization across other 3D generative models, hyperparameter ablations, and efficiency numbers are deferred to the (unavailable) Supplementary Material. The threat model is strictly white-box and assumes the defender controls the immunization/distillation step before weight release, and constructing the defense benefits from access to target-domain-like data (mitigated but not eliminated by the cross-domain robust setting). No adaptive attacker that specifically knows about and tries to neutralize the traps is evaluated.

datasets_used: Objaverse (authorized source domain; original LGM training data)
Google Scanned Objects (GSO; defense and/or attack domain)
Evaluation categories: shoe, plant, dish, bowl, box
quantitative_results: Intra-domain LoRA (Omni->Omni), shoe: GaussLock Target PSNR 13.96 / LPIPS 0.1920 flat across 100-400 steps vs baseline PSNR 23.17->24.85 (LPIPS 0.0573->0.0464)
Intra-domain LoRA, dish: GaussLock locked at 12.21 PSNR / 0.2372 LPIPS; box locked at 11.37 / 0.2878; bowl 11.28 / 0.2981
Full-parameter fine-tuning (Omni->Omni), shoe: GaussLock 13.96/0.1920 flat while naive-unlearning recovers to ~26.79 PSNR
Cross-domain LoRA (GSO->Omni), dish: GaussLock 12.20 PSNR / 0.2400 LPIPS flat vs baseline up to 26.01 PSNR
Source utility preserved: GaussLock source PSNR ~19.9-22.2 vs 22.52 unprotected baseline (e.g. shoe 21.25, plant 22.21)
Initialization ablation (Table IV, 400 attack steps): random-init target PSNR 7.67-8.27 vs pre-trained 23.20-26.03 (shoe 7.80 vs 25.03)
Extended budget (Table VII): GaussLock target PSNR 22.26 (400 steps) -> 24.15 (800) -> 25.15 (1600), approaching naive-unlearning 25.62 at 1600 steps
Single-trap ablation (Table X, shoe, 400 steps): opacity locks PSNR at 13.71 but drops source PSNR to 17.93; scale 23.43; rotation 24.90 and color 24.47 barely defend
Multi-category joint attack (GSO->Omni): 3-category shoe+dish+bowl locked at 12.62 PSNR / 0.2311 LPIPS with source PSNR 19.52
baselines_compared: Naive-unlearning (reversed task loss + source distillation loss, weight-calibrated for matched source fidelity)
